displayTitle: Read the Mold Before You Read the Film

beats.narration: Castings are radiography's home turf: whatever goes wrong as the liquid freezes is locked in the volume, where the beam looks.
Sand casting: cope over drag; metal pours down the sprue, through gates; risers feed heavy sections; cores form passages. Every feature can fail.

payload.heading: The mold: every feature a failure mode
payload.note: EVERY FEATURE IS ALSO A FAILURE MODE

beats.narration: On film: gas porosity, smooth round dark spots; shrinkage, feathery and branchy; cold shuts, smooth lines, rounded ends; hot tears, ragged at fillets.

payload.heading: Four film signatures — know on sight
payload.note: LOCATION PLUS IMAGE CHARACTER NAMES THE FLAW

beats.narration: Centrifugal spins the mold: outer wall freezes dense; slag and gas crowd the bore. Investment's thin walls invite misruns and cold shuts.

payload.heading: Two more molds, same prediction game

chips.t: Slag and gas crowd the bore

beats.narration: Before you read the film, put the part back in its mold. The casting tells you where to look. Your job is to listen.
